## Observable Framework

- Frontend built with [**Observable Framework**]{.orange} — JS-based dataviz platform
- Combines [**markdown, JavaScript, and live data**]{.blue2} in reactive notebooks
- Consumes GeoServer layers via [**WMS / WFS**]{.blue2} for live map rendering

> Full stack: [**Python**]{.orange} backend · [**JavaScript, HTML, CSS**]{.blue2} frontend

::: {.notes}
Finally, our web visualization application was developed using **Observable Framework** (formerly Observable.js). This is a JavaScript framework geared towards data visualization and analysis, which allows for the creation of interactive dashboards combining code, text, and map visualizations. We use it to display geospatial layers from our GeoServer and allow our users to explore changes in built environment across the territory.

I mentioned that our backend is entirely in Python—our frontend, on the other hand, uses **JavaScript, HTML, and CSS**.

*[Show code: WFS calls to GeoServer]*

:::

## Development & Deployment Workflow

- Develop & preview locally in a [**VSCode (Onyxia)**]{.orange} service with `npm`
- [**Git push**]{.blue2} → GitHub Actions build → auto-deploy to [**GitHub Pages**]{.green2}
- Zero server to maintain, free hosting

*Demo:* [code source of the web app](https://inseefrlab.github.io/satellite-images-webapp/)

::: {.notes}
The development workflow is as follows: changes are made within a **VSCode Onyxia service** and previewed locally using `npm`. Once the changes are approved, a **Git push automatically triggers the application build** and its deployment to **GitHub Pages**.
:::
